questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Which of the following entities are eligible to submit nominations for the UNESCO/Jikji Memory of the World Prize?

options.text: Only governments of UNESCO Member States
Both governments of Member States and international NGOs in formal relations with UNESCO
Any individual or institution involved in the preservation of documentary heritage

questions.explanation: The prize is open to nominations from the governments of Member States, in consultation with their National Commissions, as well as from international non-governmental organizations (NGOs) that maintain formal relations with UNESCO.
questions.takeaway: Eligibility for nominating candidates for the Jikji Prize is restricted to Member State governments and specific international NGOs with formal UNESCO ties.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Open to governments of Member States and international NGOs maintaining formal relations with UNESCO.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The 'Buljo jikji simche yojeol', the namesake of a UNESCO prize, is historically significant primarily because:

options.text: It is the earliest known manuscript written in the Hangul script of Korea.
It is the oldest surviving book printed using movable metal type, predating the Gutenberg Bible.
It was the first document to be inscribed on the UNESCO Memory of the World Register.
It represents the first use of woodblock printing for religious texts in East Asia.

questions.explanation: The Jikji was created in 1377, which makes it the oldest extant book printed with movable metal type. This technology predates the Gutenberg Bible (printed in the 1450s), making it a milestone in the history of printing technology.
questions.takeaway: The Jikji's significance lies in its status as the oldest extant example of movable metal type printing, a key technological precursor to modern printing.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Named after the oldest extant book printed with movable metal type, created in 1377 in Korea (Buljo jikji simche yojeol).
